methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $150M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $1.5B.

data.narrative:
Ares Industries is a Series A-stage defense and security frontier-tech company founded by Alex Tseng and Devan Plantamura, currently valued at $150M post-money on $31M total raised. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies in the Defense & Security sector—2 public-market comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers including Vannevar Labs, Anduril Industries, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI, predominantly at Series B stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression, grounded in the $150M last post-money valuation, applies a typical 2.0x Series A step-up multiplier, implying $300M. Peer-median analysis across 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.0B, with a range from $60M to $1.5B; representative Series B peers trade at $1.0B–$1.5B. These methods produce substantial variance, reflecting the dispersion in stage and maturity within the comp set.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $255M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $650M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around whether Ares will track toward the lower end of peer valuations or sustain growth momentum toward Series B-stage multiples. Key dependencies include revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and execution against defense-sector procurement cycles.
